gradeRationale: The supplied EPA records contain unresolved, non-health-based compliance items, including the Revised Total Coliform Rule and an unidentified code 2049 record. They do not report a health-based contaminant exceedance, but the unresolved and incompletely labeled records prevent a stronger grade.
summary: Saratoga Springs City is an active surface-water community system serving 26,525 people. The supplied records show both resolved and unresolved non-health-based rule or compliance violations, but no health-based contaminant-limit exceedance is identified.

contaminants.name: Consumer Confidence Report Rule
contaminants.whatItIs: A public-reporting requirement rather than a measured contaminant; the supplied violation is marked resolved.
contaminants.healthEffects: The record is classified as non-health-based and provides no contaminant concentration or health effect.

contaminants.name: Contaminant code 2049
contaminants.whatItIs: The EPA label and category are unavailable in the supplied data. Duplicate records for the same period and violation ID include two marked resolved and one marked unresolved.
contaminants.healthEffects: The records are classified as non-health-based; no health effect or measured concentration is supplied.

contaminants.name: Contaminant code 2805
contaminants.whatItIs: The EPA label and category are unavailable in the supplied data; this record is marked resolved.
contaminants.healthEffects: The record is classified as non-health-based and supplies no health-effect information.

contaminants.name: Contaminant code 2806
contaminants.whatItIs: The EPA label and category are unavailable in the supplied data; this record is marked resolved.
contaminants.healthEffects: The record is classified as non-health-based and supplies no health-effect information.

contaminants.name: Revised Total Coliform Rule
contaminants.whatItIs: A microbial-rule compliance item for June 2024. The supplied violation is marked unresolved, but no microbial test result or concentration is provided.
contaminants.healthEffects: The violation record is classified as non-health-based, so it does not establish a health-based microbial exceedance.

contaminants.name: Lead and Copper Rule Revisions
contaminants.whatItIs: A lead-and-copper regulatory requirement rather than evidence of a measured lead or copper exceedance; the supplied violation is marked resolved.
contaminants.healthEffects: The record is classified as non-health-based and provides no lead or copper measurements or health-effect finding.

filterRecommendation.reason: The supplied data do not identify a measured contaminant or health-based limit exceedance that would support choosing activated carbon or reverse osmosis. A household filter also would not correct public-reporting or regulatory-compliance records.

fish.explanation: The data do not provide water-quality measurements or identify whether chlorine or chloramine is used. Aquarium safety therefore cannot be determined; confirm the disinfectant with the utility and condition aquarium water appropriately.

plants.explanation: Plant suitability cannot be determined because hardness, salinity, disinfectant levels, and other relevant measurements are unavailable.

pets.explanation: The records do not provide contaminant concentrations sufficient to judge pet safety, and unresolved compliance items remain.
